concerns.text: The validation design is circular: FR-005 defines niche‑shift magnitude as the Euclidean distance between climate‑space centroids (ΔT, ΔP). FR-006 defines the predictor (regional warming rate) as the temperature component ΔT of the same centroid difference. Regressing the response that contains ΔT against a predictor that is ΔT (or a monotone function of it) guarantees a positive association and cannot falsify the hypothesis that species track climate change.

Euclidean distance is computed in unstandardized climate space (°C vs. mm). Because temperature and precipitation have different numeric ranges, the distance is dominated by temperature, biasing the niche‑shift metric. Standardizing (e.g., z‑scores) before distance calculation is the accepted practice in climate‑niche analyses.
